quote: Deep in the human unconscious is a pervasive need for a logical universe that makes sense. But the real universe is always one step beyond logic.
author: Frank Herbert

quote: A reliable way to make people believe in falsehoods is frequent repetition, because familiarity is not easily distinguished from truth. Authoritarian institutions and marketers have always known this fact.
author: Daniel Kahneman

quote: Hence I have no mercy or compassion in me for a society that will crush people, and then penalize them for not being able to stand up under the weight.
author: Malcolm X

quote: Freedom is a heavy load, a great and strange burden for the spirit to undertake. It is not easy. It is not a gift given, but a choice made, and the choice may be a hard one.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

quote: War doesn't determine who's right. War determines who remains.
author: R.F. Kuang

quote: What's the point of having a voice if you're gonna be silent in those moments you shouldn't be?
author: Angie Thomas

quote: Nothing is so painful to the human mind as a great and sudden change.
author: Mary Shelley

quote: You act like mortals in all that you fear, and like immortals in all that you desire.
author: Seneca

quote: Sometimes, immersed in his books, there would come to him the awareness of all that he did not know, of all that he had not read; and the serenity for which he labored was shattered as he realized the little time he had in life to read so much, to learn what he had to know.
author: John Williams

quote: You never really understand a person until you consider things from his point of view... Until you climb inside of his skin and walk around in it.
author: Harper Lee
